::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="How to Use this Page"}
The blocks on this page build one script. Paste each block into the same R
script file as you reach it, in order — by the end, the whole file runs cleanly
from top to bottom, except in the few places where a comment says an error is
deliberate (so you can see what one looks like).

A few mechanics worth knowing:

- Every block starts with a dashed comment header (`## ---- 1. ... ----`). It
  marks where one block ends and the next begins in your growing script — and
  RStudio reads those headers as sections, so they appear in the script's
  outline for easy jumping around.
- Experiment freely as you go — change a number, rerun a line, poke at things.
  Building the script yourself is the point.
- We recommend working through the blocks one at a time first -- you learn far
  more by building the script yourself and watching each piece run than by
  reading a finished file. Once you've done that (or if you'd rather grab
  everything at once), the complete, clean script -- without your experiments,
  and finished so it runs top to bottom -- is available to download at the
  bottom of the page.

This box is the same on every hands-on page in this group, so once you've read
it here, you can skip it elsewhere.
:::
